weekly.angle: The epistemological rupture: understanding why psychoanalysis requires a hermeneutic standard of evidence rather than a natural-science one.
must_cover: Ricoeurs four criteria for psychoanalytic facts: Language, Intersubjectivity, Psychic Reality, and Narrative.
The distinction between historical truth and narrative truth.
The clinical space as a non-traditional but rigorous laboratory for observation.
avoid: Soapy details of the Dora case unless directly illustrating methodological concepts.
Reducing psychoanalysis to early biological drive theory, ignoring the hermeneutic shift.
grounding: Ground epistemology in Ricoeur (1981) and Gammelgaard (2010).
Frame the Dora case as a clinical illustration of psychic reality and methodological challenge, not historical trivia.

avoid: Extensive historical context of Freuds life.

grounding_notes: Emphasize that psychoanalysis does not reject science, but rejects scientism and positivism.
Use the Dora case strictly to highlight methodological principles like transference and the limits of the analysts objectivity.
